title: gagnebina pervilleana
intro: Gagnebina pervilleana is a plant species commonly known as the "Mai Mok Mok" or "Mountain Taro," belonging to the family Celastraceae. It is native to the tropical forests of New Caledonia, and its roots are used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties. Gagnebina pervilleana has been found to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may contribute to its health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The roots of the Gagnebina pervilleana plant can be prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it is also used in herbal supplements and traditional remedies.
